_meta.name: feature-graph
_meta.status: DRAFT — vocabulary to be derived/expanded from real production SLDPRT files (see project-context). Do NOT freeze before validating against real designs.
_meta.role: CAD-NEUTRAL intermediate representation (IR). The host AI (Planner) emits this; the deterministic solidworks-compiler lowers it to tool calls. The model NEVER emits raw tool sequences.
_meta.doubles_as: Capability registry — the set of supported feature node types + params IS the capability list. The Planner validates intent against this single artifact (no separate chatty discovery protocol).

graph.schema_version: string (required — IR schema version this graph targets)
graph.units: string (fixed: 'meters' — all lengths are SI meters, matching execution layer)
graph.nodes: array of feature nodes (ordered; order defines build sequence)

node_common.id: string (required — stable node id, e.g. 'n1'; used as a reference target by later nodes)
node_common.type: string (required — feature type discriminator; must be one of the registered types below)
node_common.ref: reference object (optional — where this feature attaches; see reference_model). Omitted = canonical default datum.

reference_model._note: OPEN DESIGN QUESTION — this is the make-or-break part of the IR (persistent/topological naming). v0 supports only the cases below; expand from real designs.

node_face.node: string — id of an earlier node
node_face.selector: string — semantic face selector resolved at compile time by the reference resolver: 'top' | 'bottom' | 'side' | ...

reference_model.position: string — semantic position on the referenced entity: 'center' | 'corner' | ... (resolved to concrete coords by the resolver)

v0_exp_covered_subset._note: The EXPERIMENTAL submit_feature_graph tool (P1.4/P1.7, logs-ir.md) compiles ONLY this minimal subset today. Outside it the compiler rejects the graph at structural-validation time (no silent gaps). Grow from real MAT parts, not ahead of them.
v0_exp_covered_subset.assumptions: box is built CENTRED ON THE DATUM ORIGIN (so 'center' on its top face maps to sketch (0,0)); 'top' is bound to global +Y (SolidWorks 'up'); single body. General 3D-point->sketch-2D resolution for arbitrary faces is P1.5.

feature_types_v0._note: Profile and operation are SEPARATED on purpose (avoids combinatorial type explosion like 'extruded_circle' + 'extruded_rectangle' + 'revolved_circle'...).

box.description: Rectangular boss convenience node — a single IR node that the compiler lowers to many tool calls (create_sketch on the datum + a centred rectangle + a boss extrude). The clearest demonstration of one feature -> many ops.
box.ref: datum (the base plane the box is built on; default 'top')
box.width: number — meters (along the datum's local X)
box.depth: number — meters (along the datum's local Y)

sketch.description: A 2D profile on a plane/face. Entities are profile primitives, not tools.
sketch.profile: array of profile primitives: { kind:'rectangle', width, height } | { kind:'circle', diameter } | { kind:'polygon', sides, diameter } ...

extrude.description: Solid extrusion of a referenced sketch node.
extrude.sketch: string — id of a sketch node

revolve.description: Solid of revolution of a referenced sketch node about an axis.
revolve.sketch: string — id of a sketch node
revolve.axis: reference — axis definition (datum or sketch centerline)

hole.description: Parametric hole. Encapsulates sketch+cut; the compiler expands it (resolve top face -> create_sketch(on_face, face_index) -> circle at centre -> through cut).
hole.ref: node_face + position (e.g. top face of n1, center)
hole.depth: number — meters, or 'through_all' (v0-exp supports 'through_all' only; 'through' accepted as an alias)

validation_split.plan_time_planner: Structural + schema only: type is registered, required params present, node references (sketch/node ids) structurally resolvable, units correct.
validation_split.compile_exec_time: Geometric validity: referenced face actually exists after prior features, hole fits within the face, selectors resolve to exactly one entity. Owned by compiler + reference resolver + execution — NOT the Planner.

example._intent: Create a 20mm hole at the center of the top face of a 100x100x20mm block.

example_v0_exp._intent: A 50mm box with a 10mm hole centred on the top face, through-all (the canonical submit_feature_graph experiment fixture).
